 {"id":"bd-jnefr","title":"add mounted-path btrfs read-only EROFS coverage for fallocate mutation attempts","description":"Survey finding: btrfs fuse_e2e covers writable fallocate success and unsupported-mode rejection, but not the mounted-path read-only contract even though ffs-fuse rejects fallocate with EROFS before dispatch on read-only mounts. Scope: add btrfs read-only probes that attempt representative fallocate mutations such as preallocate and keep-size or punch-hole forms, asserting exact EROFS with unchanged apparent size and file contents. Acceptance: btrfs FUSE E2E freezes operator-visible read-only refusal for fallocate mutation attempts.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T08:33:30.790018523Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T21:11:16.033595492Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T21:11:16.031663628Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

+{"id":"bd-jqama","title":"Preserve btrfs prealloc FIEMAP across range fallocate shifts","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"cod-fs","created_at":"2026-04-26T03:33:10.765748007Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T03:53:55.675915758Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T03:53:55.673967640Z","close_reason":"Implemented btrfs collapse/insert prealloc FIEMAP preservation with tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-jrgbe","title":"Add golden formatting contract for representative ffs-types scalar displays and parse errors","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T21:35:39.800324358Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T21:44:13.722101004Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T21:44:13.719925425Z","close_reason":"Added exact golden formatting contract for representative ffs-types scalar displays and ParseError messages; verified with rch cargo test -p ffs-types representative_scalar_and_parse_error_exact_golden_contract -- --nocapture, rch cargo check --workspace --all-targets, rch cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings, and rch cargo fmt --check","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-jtt2","title":"Implement external journal paired replay (not just UUID validation)","description":"BACKGROUND: ext4 filesystems can use an external journal device (JOURNAL_DEV incompat flag on the data FS, journal_dev field points to device number, journal_uuid links the pair). When crash recovery is needed, the JBD2 journal blocks live on the external device rather than in an internal inode.

CURRENT STATE: OpenOptions::external_journal_path accepts an external journal image path. maybe_probe_external_journal opens it, reads its JBD2 superblock, validates UUID pairing and block size match, and stores ExternalJournalInfo on OpenFs. But maybe_replay_ext4_journal short-circuits when journal_dev != 0 — it logs 'journal replay skipped' and returns Ok(None). No actual replay occurs.

The log message honestly states: 'external journal UUID validation only; full paired replay not yet implemented'.

IMPLEMENTATION PLAN:
1. When journal_dev != 0 AND external_journal_path is Some AND UUIDs match:
   a. Open the external journal image as a ByteDevice.
   b. Parse the JBD2 superblock from the external device.
   c. Compute journal segments: the entire external device (minus superblock) is the journal area.
   d. Feed the segments to replay_jbd2_segments the same way we do for internal journals.
   e. The replay writes go to the DATA filesystem's device, not the journal device.
2. When journal_dev != 0 AND external_journal_path is None AND crash recovery IS needed:
   - Currently returns UnsupportedFeature error (good — fail-fast).
   - No change needed here.
3. When journal_dev != 0 AND external_journal_path is None AND crash recovery NOT needed:
   - Currently returns Ok(None) with info log (good — graceful skip).
   - No change needed here.

KEY CONSIDERATIONS:
- The external journal's block layout is different from an internal journal. The JBD2 superblock is at block 0 of the device, and journal blocks start immediately after. There's no inode or extent mapping.
- We need to compute JournalSegment { start, blocks } for the external device based on the JBD2 superblock's s_maxlen field.
- The replay must write to the data device, not the journal device. The existing replay infrastructure takes a BlockDevice for writes — we just need to pass the data device.
- After successful replay, we should clear the needs_recovery flag on the data device's superblock.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- A test creates a synthetic data FS + external journal pair, dirties the journal, and verifies replay applies blocks to the data device.
- The log message changes from 'UUID validation only' to 'external journal replay completed'.
- UUID mismatch still fails fast.
- Block size mismatch still fails fast.
- ExternalJournalInfo is populated with journal metadata after successful replay.
- The 'required crash recovery' error path still works when no external path is provided.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:05:40.642853327Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:42:00.763994355Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:42:00.763527321Z","close_reason":"All subtasks complete. External journal paired replay is fully implemented: UUID validation, segment computation (bd-vdk6), and replay wiring (bd-vql1) are all done. 4 tests covering success path, UUID mismatch, dirty FS requirement, and clean FS skip.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ext4","journal","journal_dev"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-jtt2","depends_on_id":"bd-vdk6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:10:55.294301302Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-jtt2","depends_on_id":"bd-vql1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:10:56.821268230Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-juy7","title":"Add ext4 inode checksum adversarial fuzz regression coverage","description":"Fallback testing-fuzzing parity round: add deterministic adversarial corpus coverage for ext4 inode checksum stamping/verification, corruption rejection, and size-boundary failures in crates/ffs-ondisk/tests/adversarial_corpus.rs; update conformance/COVERAGE.md.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T06:36:09.623765671Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T06:51:33.215554859Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T06:51:33.213412544Z","close_reason":"Added ext4 inode checksum adversarial fuzz regression coverage","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ext4","fuzz","ondisk","testing"]}

@@ -4286,12 +4286,16 @@ at boundary, deallocate inner blocks, zero partial boundary blocks. Update
 
 **FALLOC_FL_COLLAPSE_RANGE:** Remove range and shift subsequent data down.
 Rewrites extent tree mappings. Range MUST be block-aligned (`EINVAL`).
+Shifted preallocated/unwritten extents MUST remain preallocated/unwritten at
+their new logical offsets, including FIEMAP `UNWRITTEN` observability.
 
 **FALLOC_FL_ZERO_RANGE:** Zero range without deallocation. Allocated blocks
 get zeroes written. Unallocated blocks marked as uninitialized extents.
 
 **FALLOC_FL_INSERT_RANGE:** Insert hole at offset, shift data up. Inverse of
-collapse. Range MUST be block-aligned.
+collapse. Range MUST be block-aligned. The inserted range is a logical hole;
+shifted preallocated/unwritten extents MUST retain their unwritten FIEMAP
+contract rather than being materialized as plain holes.
 
 Block allocation strategy: goal set to physical block after file's last
 extent for contiguity. Large allocations (>8 MB) use buddy allocator

@@ -232,6 +232,8 @@ behavior of that operation, not whether the row is unimplemented.
 | `unit::btrfs_write_fallocate_zero_range_keep_size_does_not_extend_file` | `fallocate` with `FALLOC_FL_ZERO_RANGE|FALLOC_FL_KEEP_SIZE` | supported | success, file size unchanged when range extends past EOF |
 | `unit::btrfs_write_fallocate_collapse_range_shifts_tail_and_shrinks_file` | `fallocate` with `FALLOC_FL_COLLAPSE_RANGE` | supported | success, aligned range is removed, tail data shifts left, and file size shrinks |
 | `unit::btrfs_write_fallocate_insert_range_shifts_tail_and_grows_file` | `fallocate` with `FALLOC_FL_INSERT_RANGE` | supported | success, aligned zero hole is inserted, tail data shifts right, and file size grows |
+| `unit::btrfs_write_fallocate_collapse_range_preserves_shifted_prealloc_fiemap` | `FALLOC_FL_COLLAPSE_RANGE` shifting a prealloc extent | supported | success, shifted prealloc remains allocated and reports `FIEMAP_EXTENT_UNWRITTEN` at its new logical offset |
+| `unit::btrfs_write_fallocate_insert_range_preserves_shifted_prealloc_fiemap` | `FALLOC_FL_INSERT_RANGE` shifting a prealloc extent | supported | success, inserted range is a hole while shifted prealloc remains allocated and reports `FIEMAP_EXTENT_UNWRITTEN` |
 | `unit::btrfs_write_fallocate_collapse_range_reaching_eof_rejects_without_mutation` | `FALLOC_FL_COLLAPSE_RANGE` reaching EOF | supported | `EINVAL`, file size and data preserved |
 | `unit::btrfs_write_fallocate_insert_range_at_eof_rejects_without_mutation` | `FALLOC_FL_INSERT_RANGE` at EOF | supported | `EINVAL`, file size and data preserved |
 | `unit::btrfs_write_fallocate_range_modes_reject_unaligned_with_einval_without_mutation` | exclusive range modes with unaligned offset/length | supported | `EINVAL`, file size and data preserved |

@@ -1364,8 +1364,8 @@ See [FEATURE_PARITY.md](FEATURE_PARITY.md) for the full capability matrix and [P
 | `fallocate` (`mode=0`, `FALLOC_FL_KEEP_SIZE`) | Partially supported | Preallocation paths are supported and validated |
 | `fallocate` (`FALLOC_FL_PUNCH_HOLE\|FALLOC_FL_KEEP_SIZE`) | Supported (experimental) | Success path zero-fills the requested range while preserving file size and unaffected bytes |
 | `fallocate` (`FALLOC_FL_ZERO_RANGE`, optional `FALLOC_FL_KEEP_SIZE`) | Supported (experimental) | Success path zero-fills the requested range; `KEEP_SIZE` preserves EOF while non-`KEEP_SIZE` can extend file size |
-| `fallocate` (`FALLOC_FL_COLLAPSE_RANGE`) | Supported (experimental) | Success path removes the requested aligned range, shifts the tail left, and shrinks the file size |
-| `fallocate` (`FALLOC_FL_INSERT_RANGE`) | Supported (experimental) | Success path inserts an aligned hole, shifts the tail right, and grows the file size |
+| `fallocate` (`FALLOC_FL_COLLAPSE_RANGE`) | Supported (experimental) | Success path removes the requested aligned range, shifts the tail left, shrinks the file size, and preserves shifted prealloc extents as FIEMAP `UNWRITTEN` |
+| `fallocate` (`FALLOC_FL_INSERT_RANGE`) | Supported (experimental) | Success path inserts an aligned hole, shifts the tail right, grows the file size, and preserves shifted prealloc extents as FIEMAP `UNWRITTEN` |
 | `fallocate` (unknown/extra mode bits) | Unsupported | Must return `EOPNOTSUPP` (`FfsError::UnsupportedFeature`) with no partial data/size mutation before rejection |
 | Unsupported-path observability | Required | Structured logs include `operation_id`, `scenario_id`, `outcome`, and `error_class` |
